facts_disclosed: Spent $2K on gaming PC — contradiction with saving

turns.content: dude I gotta tell somebody about this cuz im wicked hyped rn I just finished buildin my new gaming pc
Nice, building your own PC is always exciting! What specs did you go with?

bro ok so I went with a 4070 for the gpu which was the main thing I wanted and then I got a ryzen 7 7700x 32 gigs of ram and a 1tb nvme ssd. the whole build came out to like 2 grand with the new monitor I picked up too

That's a really solid build. The 4070 is a beast for 1440p gaming and the 7700X pairs perfectly with it -- you won't be bottlenecked at all. 32 gigs of RAM is the sweet spot right now too. What monitor did you pick up?
I got a 27 inch 1440p 165hz from gigabyte its so clean bro like comin from my old 1080p 60hz monitor its literally a different world everything looks so smooth I cant stop starin at it

Oh man, the jump from 1080p 60Hz to 1440p 165Hz is probably the biggest visual upgrade you can make. Everything looks sharper and once you see high refresh rate you literally cannot go back. That Gigabyte monitor is a great pick for the price too. What games are you playing on it?

first thing I loaded up was warzone and bro im gettin like 120 fps on high settings it used to run like garbage on my old setup I was gettin like 40 fps on low. its a completely different game now I can actually see people across the map

Going from 40 fps on low to 120 on high is a massive jump -- you're basically playing a different game at that point. With 165Hz and those frames, your movement and aiming should feel way more responsive too. Your K/D is about to go up for sure.

thats what im sayin bro I was already decent but now I feel like I got an advantage. also tried the new cod campaign on it and the graphics are insane on this thing. ray tracing on and its still pushin like 90 fps
The 4070 handles ray tracing really well, especially with DLSS turned on. 90 fps with ray tracing in the campaign is legit. Did you build the whole thing yourself or did someone help you out?

nah I did it all myself I watched like 30 youtube videos before I started lol I was wicked nervous puttin the cpu in cuz the pins looked so fragile but it went in smooth. honestly the hardest part was gettin the io shield lined up everything else was pretty straightforward

That's impressive for a first build. The CPU install is always the scariest part but once it drops in you realize it's fine. And yeah, the IO shield is weirdly one of the most annoying parts of any build -- such a simple thing but it never wants to cooperate. What case did you go with?
I got the nzxt h5 flow its white and I threw some rgb fans in there so it looks sick on my desk. the cable management in the back is kinda ugly but you cant see it so whatever

The H5 Flow is a great airflow case and the white with RGB is a super clean look. Cable management behind the panel is for your eyes only -- as long as nothing is blocking airflow and the side panel closes, you're good. For $2K all-in including the monitor, you actually did really well.

yeah I feel like I got wicked good value honestly. I was lookin at prebuilts and they wanted like 1800 just for the tower with worse specs so buildin it myself saved me a ton and I got a better machine

That's the main advantage of building -- you get exactly what you want and save money doing it. Prebuilts often cheap out on the power supply or motherboard to hit a price point. Yours should last you a solid 3-4 years at high settings before you'd even think about upgrading the GPU.

thats the plan I dont wanna have to upgrade again anytime soon so I figured just do it right the first time you know. my roommate danny keeps comin in my room and just starin at it hes still on a ps5 so hes wicked jealous

Ha, that's always the best part -- watching your console friends see a proper PC setup for the first time. He's probably going to start looking into building one himself now. And you're right, spending a little more upfront to get the 4070 means you won't be itching to upgrade in a year.
I already told him im helpin him build one if he wants cuz now I know how to do it lol. might even stream on this thing too I feel like its powerful enough to game and stream at the same time

With a 7700X and 4070, you can absolutely game and stream simultaneously. The 4070 has a great NVENC encoder so you can use that for streaming without it touching your CPU performance at all. OBS is free and pretty easy to set up if you want to try it out.
oh word I didnt know that. im def gonna try that out this weekend. alright bro I gotta go im about to hop on warzone and break this thing in for real preciate you lettin me brag about it lol
Go enjoy it man, you earned it after building the thing yourself. That first real gaming weekend on a new rig hits different. Have fun out there!
